title: The Beekeeper of Aleppo
description:
The Beekeeper of Aleppo had its world premiere at Nottingham Playhouse in early 2023 and went on to tour the UK, receiving rave reviews wherever it played.

From the producers behind the hugely successful West End and Broadway stage adaptation of The Kite Runner.

Nuri is a beekeeper, his wife, Afra, an artist. They live a simple life, rich in family and friends, in the beautiful Syrian city of Aleppo – until the unthinkable happens.

When all they care for is destroyed by war, they are forced to escape. On their terrifying journey, they must face the pain of their own unbearable loss alongside incredible danger. Above all, they must journey to find each other again. This compassionate and beautiful play is a story of connection – between friends, families, and strangers.

“A story of loss, love, resilience and hope. … it’s impossible not to be moved.” The Observer

Winner of the Aspen Words Award, Runner up for the Dayton Literary Peace Prize, and one of The Times top three bestselling books of 2020, Christy Lefteri’s best-selling novel is adapted by Nesrin Alrefaai and Matthew Spangler, who adapted Broadway and West End hit The Kite Runner.

Martin Dodd for UK Productions Ltd present the Nottingham Playhouse Production of THE BEEKEEPER OF ALEPPO

Directed by ANTHONY ALMEIDA

Estimated run time: 2 hrs 20 mins

- BSL Sign Interpreted: There will be someone (usually stood on the front right hand corner of the stage) "signing" the performance in British Sign Language. They will sign characters speeches through the production to allow those with loss of hearing to follow the play.

- Audio Described: Audio description is a live verbal commentary providing information on the visual elements of a production as it unfolds.

“Sparklingly assured… impressive show… anchored in strong performances from a uniformly excellent ensemble cast” - The Telegraph

“you can’t fail to be moved by it.” - Manchester Evening News
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: The Illegal Eagles
description:
In 2026, the Illegal Eagles celebrate an incredible 30 years on the road with their brand-new Hotel California Tour.

Renowned for their flawless vocals, stunning guitars, and an uncanny ability to recreate the legendary sound of the Eagles, the Illegal Eagles have earned a reputation as one of the most accomplished and popular live acts in the UK.

This milestone tour will feature a full performance of the iconic Hotel California album, played in its entirety — including classics like Life in the Fast Lane, New Kid in Town, and, of course, the unforgettable title track.

Alongside this, audiences can expect a set packed with all the greatest hits from across the Eagles’ timeless back catalogue.

With decades of experience, outstanding musicianship, and a deep love for the music they perform, the Illegal Eagles promise an unforgettable evening of nostalgia and pure musical magic that continues to captivate fans old and new.

If you love Entertainment, see what else we have coming to Blackpool Grand Theatre this season HERE

Estimated run time: **2 hrs 20 mins**
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: An Evening with Jimmy Tarbuck
description:
Jimmy Tarbuck: “Laughter is the only drug I’ve ever needed”

JIMMY TARBUCK OBE, one of the most popular household names from the golden era of British stand up, is back with a bang:

The much-loved entertainer is on tour through the spring and summer 2026 with his solo show ‘An Evening with Jimmy Tarbuck’ which stops off at some of his favourite towns and venues in the UK. And he can’t wait.

Jimmy says:

“It’s been great, and I love getting on stage, where I belong. Now I am really looking forward to visiting the towns and cities where I learned my craft and built my fanbase. The crowds are always on great form and always ready to laugh. And laughter is the only drug I’ve ever needed!”

‘An Evening with Jimmy Tarbuck’ will see the acclaimed comedian and raconteur reflecting on a remarkable life in showbusiness, the seeds of which were sown in childhood.

Jimmy will present a hugely entertaining mix of anecdotes and jokes, including recollections of his many years presenting Sunday Night at the London Palladium which shot him to fame in 1963.

There will also be stories from Jimmy’s childhood, growing up in Liverpool where he was classmates – and good mates – with John Lennon.

And of course, his golfing life, which has proved to be a rich source of entertainment and celebrity both on and off the course.

Jimmy’s incredible career has taken him around the world, mixing and befriending the biggest stars in entertainment and sport: he’s played golf with Arnold Palmer, football with Bobby Moore, sang with Tom Jones, partied with Parky and Lynchy, drank with Frank (Sinatra) and sipped champagne with Cilla; and he treasures his time working with the legendary Judy Garland, who affectionately called him her ‘Little Man’. Fans will be treated to all these anecdotes and more, by one of our true national treasures.

If you love Entertainment, see what else we have coming to Blackpool Grand Theatre this season here.

Estimated run time: 2 hrs 0 mins
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: Lost In Music
description:
LOST IN MUSIC – The Original Disco Show!

Embark on an electrifying journey through the sensational 70s. Our world class band and dazzling vocals are set to transport you straight to the heart of disco fever.

Get ready to re-live timeless hits from icons like Donna Summer, Gloria Gaynor, Earth, Wind & Fire, Sister Sledge and Chic.

Dress to impress in your most fabulous disco attire as we pay homage to the golden age of disco. From the irresistible beats of “Never Can Say Goodbye” to the pulsating rhythm of “Boogie Wonderland,” we’ve got an unforgettable lineup that’ll keep you grooving all night long.

Don’t miss out on the feel-good show of the year – join us, lose yourself in the music, and leave your worries behind!

Lost in Music – touring the nation, tickets on sale now!

This is a tribute show and is in no way affiliated with any original artists/estates/management companies or similar shows.

Promoter reserves the right to alter the programme.

Estimated run time: 2 hrs 20 mins
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: Breakin’ Convention
description:
The groundbreaking festival of hip hop dance theatre from around the world, and around the corner, returns to Blackpool Grand.

Prepare to be blown away by high-energy, world-class performances from celebrated poppers, lockers, b-boys and b-girls.

Get ready for the mighty TRAPLORD, the Olivier Award-winning creation from Ivan Michael Blackstock – a visionary creative behind choreography for Beyoncé and other global names. Exploring masculinity and mental health, TRAPLORD is a powerful fusion of movement and storytelling which challenges racial stereotypes of Black men.

Next up, Low Air (Lithuania) – Lithuania’s first independent urban dance company, established in 2012 by creators Airida Gudaitė and Laurynas Žakevičius. Based in Vilnius, it blends street dance with contemporary performance to foster community and educate youth, earning numerous national awards and international recognition.

Also in this year’s lineup is Femme Fatale (France/Mexico/South Korea), a mesmerising female trio known for their musicality, precision, and technique.

All this, plus incredible local talent from House of Wingz, Freefly crew ft Sam and Aysh De Belle, and 0161 Studios.

Breakin’ Convention is a total hip hop takeover, arrive early (17:30) for free pre-show activities like cyphers, workshops, graffiti and DJs. The vibe hits the moment you walk in.

Curated and hosted by UK hip hop theatre legend and Breakin’ Convention Artistic Director Jonzi D, this dynamic celebration of hip hop culture will fill the entire venue from stage to foyer with live DJs, graffiti artists, freestyle dance sessions, and much more.

Since 2004, Breakin’ Convention, based at Sadler’s Wells Theatre in London, has led the global hip hop theatre movement, showcasing leading artists and connecting communities through the power of dance, music, and storytelling.

Breakin’ Convention Festival – Parental Guidance recommended. This age guidance is at the discretion of the accompanying Parent/ Guardian. Above all, Breakin’ Convention Festival is a celebration of the best hip hop has to offer, highlighting incredible artists from all over the world and we look forward to welcoming you.
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: Desperate Scousewives
description:
Desperate Scousewives starring Crissy Rock

Get ready for a riotous, fast-paced comedy following the lives of three loud, no-nonsense Liverpool women and one nervous newcomer, all navigating life, love, loyalty, and lipstick on a tightly knit street.

Susan, Vanessa and Lily dominate the street with their big personalities, big voices, and even bigger secrets.

But everything is turned upside down with the arrival of Trisha, a single mum from Manchester, thrown into the lion’s den of long-established friendships and fierce loyalty.

Starring TV comedy legend Crissy Rock (Benidorm, I’m A Celebrity) and the shows writer and director Lynne Fitzgerald. Desperate Scousewives is packed with unforgettable one-liners, high heels, heartbreak and hilarity. Not for the faint-hearted!

If you love Comedy, see what else we have coming to Blackpool Grand Theatre this season HERE

title: Les Ballets Trockadero de Monte Carlo
description:
For 50 years, the all-male comedy ballet company Les Ballets Trockadero de Monte Carlo (affectionately known as “The Trocks”) has delighted audiences of all ages with sell-out performances featuring their fabulous sense of fun and flawless dancing.

“This is the funniest night you’ll ever have at the ballet.” The Guardian

Originally from New York, the Trocks are now loved worldwide for their sassy spoofs and hilarious homages to classical ballet, where the dancers each transform into two personas, both ballerina and danseur!

Every performance frivolously froths with tutus and testosterone, blush-pink ballet pumps, fierce false eyelashes and prima ballerina attitude. However, what makes this company extraordinarily special is their immaculate technique and daring physicality, surpassed only by their impeccable comic timing.

“The Trocks deliver a kick from a steel toe-cap in a silky pointe shoe.” Daily Telegraph

“We came to laugh, but we stayed to worship” The Telegraph

“One of the most unusually gifted dance companies in existence” The Stage

“People come for the laughs, but stay for the dancing” The Scotsman

TOUR PROGRAMME

Swan Lake; Pas de Deux or modern work or solo to be announced; Go For Barocco; Dying Swan; Walpurgisnacht

Swan Lake – Choreography after Lev Ivanovich Ivanov. Music by Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky.

The Trocks’ version of what is probably the best-known ballet in the world is an elegiac phantasmagoria of variations and ensembles in line and music. Swept up into the magical realm of swans (and birds), it is the company’s signature work.

Pas de Deux or modern work or solo to be announced

Go For Barocco – Choreography by Peter Anastos. Music by JS Bach.

Billed as the stylistic heir to Balanchine’s “Middle-Blue-Verging-On-Black-and-White Period,” Go For Barocco has become a primer in identifying stark coolness and choreosymphonic delineation in the new (neo) neo-new classic dance!

Dying Swan – Choreography after Michel Fokine. Music by Camille Saint-Saens.

It’s probably no exaggeration to say that the Trocks’ version of Dying Swan, the solo created for Anna Pavlova in 1905, has become as iconic as the original.

Walpurgisnacht – Choreography by Elena Kunikova after Leonid Lavrovsky. Music by Charles Gounod

Inspired by the Bolshoi Ballet’s Valpurgeyeva Noch, the Trocks offer their version of the lavish ballet revelling in its dramatic, supernatural, mythological themes to joyful effect.

title: Theatre Heritage Tour – Backstage Secrets (June)
description:
Backstage Secrets Tour – Stand on the raked stage and discover the backstage workings of theatre.

(Tour includes Stalls, Dress Circle, Walkway, Royal Box, Stage Door, Fly Floor, Gallery, Dressing Rooms, Matcham Court & Stage)

This tour ends with delicious cake and tea in the Dress Circle Bar.

There is no theatre quite like The Grand. The published descriptions in 1894 said so, and contemporary theatregoers agree. This is renowned architect Frank Matcham’s masterpiece theatre, with a long and lively history.

Why not explore our YouTube video with Hayley Kay who explored the theatre on a Heritage Tour with one of our volunteers. Travel from 1894 to the present day and learn more about our theatre architect Frank Matcham and his masterpiece that recently celebrated 125 Years. Watch here.

Cant make this date? See all available tour dates HERE
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: Murderous Minds with Emma Kenny
description:
Emma Kenny, the nation’s trusted expert on criminal psychology, is embarking on a brand-new live tour with Murderous Minds—a captivating show delving into the most notorious British murder cases of our time.

Emma has already wowed audiences with two sell out shows ‘The Serial Killer Next Door’ and ‘Killer Couples’.

Murderous Minds isn’t just a retelling of crimes—it’s an unflinching journey into the psyche of killers, exploring the dark motives and life circumstances that led them to commit unthinkable acts.

Known for her work dissecting real-life cases on platforms like ITV’s This Morning and her popular YouTube channel, Emma brings her unique ability to balance chilling facts with compassionate storytelling, ensuring the audience leaves informed, fascinated, and questioning everything they thought they knew about human nature.

Emma has over one million subscribers to her YouTube and Facebook and has appeared on over 100 TV shows.

Emma Kenny outsells all other crime commentators in the UK, she is a household name, a trusted source and a digital influencer with a reach unlike any other qualified commentator in the UK.

If you love Entertainment, see what else we have coming to Blackpool Grand Theatre this season HERE.

title: We Will Rock You
description:
Get ready to turn it up. For one week only, the Blackpool Grand Theatre is going to rock as the award-winning BFLOC unleashes the worldwide smash hit musical We Will Rock You by Queen and Ben Elton.

BFLOC has earned its reputation as one of the region’s most exciting and accomplished amateur companies, known for taking on ambitious titles and delivering them with heart, scale and serious quality. This is a company powered by passion and precision. When they tackle a show as iconic as We Will Rock You, you can expect something bold, joyful and unmissable.

Set in a future where live music has been silenced, and originality is under threat, the story follows a group of outsiders known as the Bohemians as they rise up against the slick, soulless Globalsoft corporation and its formidable leader, Killer Queen. It’s a story about finding your voice, standing out and letting the music back in, and it lands just as powerfully now as it did when the show first opened at London’s Dominion Theatre in 2002.

That production went on to become the longest-running show in the Dominion’s history, before thrilling more than 20 million people across 28 countries. Now it’s Blackpool’s turn, but only for a limited run.

Fuelled by 24 of Queen’s greatest anthems, this is HIT after HIT after HIT. From the opening stomp of We Will Rock You to the spine-tingling harmonies of Bohemian Rhapsody, the infectious joy of Don’t Stop Me Now and the triumphant roar of We Are The Champions, every moment is designed to lift the roof.

Big voices. Big energy. Big heart. A landmark musical brought to life by a company at the top of its game, but you have just one week to be part of it.

The award-winning BFLOC presents this amateur production of We Will Rock You by arrangement with Theatrical Rights Worldwide.
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: Roald Dahl’s Matilda The Musical JR.
description:
Welcome to Matilda JR, a brilliant musical adventure brought to life by the talented students at Theatrix UK!

THIS SHOW IS PERFORMED IN BLACKPOOL GRAND THEATRE STUDIO

Step into a world of imagination, courage, and a little bit of mischief as our young cast share the story of an extraordinary girl who dares to stand up, speak out, and change her own destiny.

Audiences can expect high-energy songs, lively choreography, and plenty of heart as these iconic moments leap from page to stage.

Whether you love the story, the songs, or simply a feel-good afternoon at the theatre, Matilda JR promises bravery, inspiration, and a celebration of friendship. Don’t miss this magical musical experience!

Theatrix UK: Drama and Musical Theatre proudly invites you to join us for Matilda JR — where imagination is powerful, kindness matters, and every child has the chance to shine.

“Even if you’re little, you can do a lot.”

title: The Carpenters Story
description:
A musical journey through the glittering career of pop’s most famous brother and sister duo.

This highly acclaimed production continues to captivate audiences across the UK with its spectacular celebration of the classic songbook that made The Carpenters a legend in the world of popular music, selling over 100 million albums and singles.

This brand new show features the outstanding vocal talents of Claire Furley, with Musical Director Phil Aldridge leading The Carpenters Story band as they re-create Richard Carpenter’s original orchestral arrangements.

Featuring all of the Carpenters hit songs including Close To You, Top of the World, We’ve Only Just Begun, Rainy Days & Mondays, Goodbye to Love and many more!

title: Return To The Forest
description:
A museum, a heist and a magical forest that comes alive through dance, puppetry and play – inviting us to reflect on what we treasure and how we connect…

Precious treasures are on display in a museum, but remember – strictly no touching! Dusk falls, and the museum is getting ready to close, but something is beginning to stir. Only a few visitors remain. These precious objects seem to call to them, yearning to be freed… Is this a heist happening in front of your eyes? Can it really be a heist when the objects are leading the way?

Celebrating their 30th birthday in 2026, Theatre-Rites are renowned for creating playful and meaningful productions for children and adults alike. For Return to the Forest, they collaborate once again with the award-winning South African Choreographer Gregory Maqoma.

Led by Maqoma’s high-energy movement and the magical puppetry of Director Sue Buckmaster (Chotto Desh), Return to the Forest journeys from a museum into the heart of a wild and magical forest, full of life and wonder. Who will we meet along the way?

After captivating audiences across the world, these incredible artists will team up for an playful and thought-provoking encounter. Combining dance and puppetry to dazzling effect, Return to the Forest is an invitation to imagine what is precious and how we care for it.

“Theatre-Rites, a company of genuine originality and flair” The Guardian

A Theatre-Rites and Factory International co-production, co-commissioned by Sadler’s Wells. Created as an immersive production for Manchester and London and adapted by Theatre-Rites for Blackpool Grand’s stage.

Estimated run time: 1 hrs 30 mins
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: Think Floyd
description:
Think Floyd’s exciting new 2026 production features The Dark Side of the Moon and Wish You Were Here—two of the most iconic and influential albums of all time.

Both will be performed in their entirety, alongside a rich selection of classic Pink Floyd tracks, spanning from the Syd Barrett era to The Division Bell.

More than just a tribute act, Think Floyd are known for their stunning musicianship, authentic sound, and meticulous attention to detail. Their performances capture the essence of Pink Floyd with passion and precision, supported by an immersive laser light show that elevates the concert into a true audiovisual experience.

Even Pink Floyd’s own Nick Mason has praised them, calling Think Floyd “brilliant” and remarking in an LBC interview that they’re “better than we are.” With such acclaim and dedication to the craft, Think Floyd’s 2026 tour promises a must-see experience for fans old and new.

“The band just gets better and better. The light show has got bigger and matches the big sound. Fully immersive show from start to finish. Thank you once again for a brilliant experience.”

“The light show is as impressive as their music.”

“Simply the best Floyd tribute!”

“An amazing show from start to finish.”

“It is almost incomprehensible to me that Think Floyd are able to play seemingly any Floyd track and make it sound so awesome, so authentic, so note perfect. Genuinely, you could close your eyes and easily imagine yourself in the company of the original artist.”

“The brilliance of the band, the lighting crew and sound engineers make every gig an outstanding Floyd experience.”

“I saw Pink Floyd several times from 1970 onwards and have to say that Think Floyd are superb.”

“A awesome show from start to finish brilliant.”

“Absolutely brilliant show! - Masters of your craft!”
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom

title: AVR Dance Show
description:
AVR is thrilled to be back with their fourth yearly appearance at The Grand Theatre.

After a hugely successful run the previous three years, AVR is excited to present another high energy show, packed full of more than 150 talented students from both the Dance and Theatre school, showcasing a mixture of Dancing, Singing and Acting.

‘The Show’ is a compilation of excerpts from much loved musicals including, Hercules, High School Musical, Shrek and Descendants in addition to Swan Lake and a story of life told through David Bowie’s greatest hits.

The perfect experience for any musical, dance or drama lover or budding youngster that loves to dance and sing.

title: Heritage Tour & Afternoon Tea – 5th July 2026
description:
Overview

Explore the Magic of The Grand

Be guided through the stunning Blackpool Grand Theatre by our knowledgeable Heritage Guides. Discover fascinating stories, hidden gems, and architectural marvels as you journey through this historic masterpiece. Learn about the theatre’s vibrant past, its artistic legacy, and the vision of its renowned architect, Frank Matcham.

An Afternoon of Elegance

After the tour, step onto The Stage for a delightful Afternoon Tea. Savor delicious treats and fine teas while seated on The Stage.

A Unique and Memorable Experience

This is more than just a tour; it’s a journey through time, a celebration of heritage, and an afternoon of indulgence. Whether you’re a theatre enthusiast, a history buff, or simply looking for a unique experience, our Theatre Tours and Afternoon Tea offer something truly special.

Afternoon tea on stands and served to each table.

Cucumber sandwiches

Cream cheese & black pepper sandwiches

Cheese and pickle sandwiches

Honey roasted ham and wholegrain mustard sandwiches

Handmade sausage rolls

Pork Pies

Selection of quiches

Fruit scones

Victoria sponge cake

Macaroons

Tea and coffee and vintage teacups and saucers

Vegan/vegetarian option available on request.

Please Note: Each table has 10 seats, and unless you purchase all the seating on the table, you will be sharing the table with other customers.

Book your tickets now and step into the enchanting world of Blackpool Grand Theatre!

Discounts & Offers

Why not make your afternoon extra special by adding a glass of fizz for just an extra £6pp.

title: Preston Dance College Summer Showcase
description:
Experience an electrifying evening of dance as Preston College Dance (PCD) present their summer showcase ‘Fragments’.

This dynamic performance brings together dancers studying on the Dance Performance and Teaching Diploma course, and on the BA (Hons) Dance Performance, Teaching and Choreography programme.

There is a vibrant programme of Commercial, Contemporary, Jazz, Lyrical, Musical Theatre, Ballet and more.

Each piece represents a fragment of the artistic journey our students undertake during their training. Choreographed by members of the PCD faculty alongside an exciting range of guest choreographers, the showcase highlights the diverse voices and creative approaches that shape the PCD experience.

Bold, expressive and full of energy, ‘Fragments’ offers a powerful snapshot of emerging talent and the passion that drives the next generation of dance artists.

Join us for an inspiring celebration of movement, creativity and performance.
address: Blackpool Grand Theatre, 33 Church St, Blackpool FY1 1HT, United Kingdom
